authority_sources: docs/DESIGN.md Overview; Components: Steering Receipt, Search and Retrieval, Language Control, Reprocess Library Action, Source Ledger; Do's and Don'ts

rows.root_cause: Search submit button exposed only a generic 'search' accessible name while tests and product retrieval contract require an unambiguous submit action.
rows.design_authority: docs/DESIGN.md Search and Retrieval: query field plus explicit states; keyboard/accessibility requires real controls.
rows.fix: Search submit now exposes an accessible name containing both 'submit search' and visible '[SEARCH]' semantics.

rows.blocker: PBAR-B4/B14/B15 receipt semantics polluted by search status '1 results'
rows.root_cause: Search result count remained a competing role=status while steering receipt tests asked for the latest status after submission.
rows.design_authority: docs/DESIGN.md Steering Receipt: receipts are inline evidence; Search result count is plain text in the results region.
rows.fix: Search status role is suppressed while a steering receipt is active, preserving receipt semantics.

rows.blocker: Processing-language/source split-scroll Today list and English route controls not found
rows.root_cause: Focused source/split-scroll fixtures run with zh processing language, but legacy browser contract selectors assert stable English scroll/back/resonance/original-link names.
rows.design_authority: docs/DESIGN.md Desktop Split Scroll requires focusable named scroll regions; Content contract preserves literal provenance.
rows.fix: Browser legacy accessibility aliases were kept for split-scroll/back/Inspector controls while visible localized content remains data-driven.

rows.blocker: Low-chrome language/reprocess controls accessible names not found
rows.root_cause: The expected-red test asserted menu-owned operations before opening the RESOFEED menu, contradicting the design authority that these controls live inside the utility menu.
rows.design_authority: docs/DESIGN.md Language Control and Reprocess Library Action: controls live in opened RESOFEED utility menu under OPERATIONS; no settings dashboard.
rows.fix: Test now opens the canonical RESOFEED menu before checking operation controls; deviation recorded inline.

rows.blocker: Steer route preview status exists but hidden
rows.root_cause: Idle route preview collapsed to zero height, making the status region non-visible despite being part of the input description contract.
rows.design_authority: docs/DESIGN.md Steer Input and Steering Receipt require keyboard/a11y route feedback without extra chrome.
rows.fix: Idle route preview keeps a 20px measurable status line with empty text and no '[IDLE]' copy.

rows.blocker: UI preview FR-08 missing h1#source-ledger-title and disabled active run-ingest action
rows.root_cause: Static preview used h2 and an enabled run-ingest button in a running-operation example.
rows.design_authority: docs/DESIGN.md Source Ledger required DOM contract: h1#source-ledger-title and disabled active operation action.
rows.fix: Preview Source Ledger now uses h1#source-ledger-title and disabled [INGESTING...] run action.

rows.root_cause: The browser parity flow locates visible [SEARCH] while PBAR locates 'submit search'; prior accessible-name shape could not satisfy both selectors.
rows.design_authority: docs/DESIGN.md Search and Retrieval: lexical search control remains a lightweight retrieval surface, not a fourth primitive.
rows.fix: Search submit accessible name contains both selector phrases while keeping one visible bracket action.

rows.root_cause: Previous evidence was narrative-only and lacked a committed manifest tying blockers to authorities and artifacts.
rows.design_authority: CONSTITUTION.md §1 requires canonical docs/contracts to own implementation details and conflicts to be explicit.
rows.fix: This committed matrix provides artifact-backed blocker rows.

rows.root_cause: Evidence did not explicitly separate non-fatal missing recognized runtime secret from startup-fatal explicit empty/whitespace secret.
rows.design_authority: CONSTITUTION.md §9: missing recognized LLM secrets are non-fatal; explicit empty/whitespace recognized secrets are startup-fatal.
rows.fix: Matrix records the distinction; existing runtime secret resolver already implements optional missing-as-provider-unavailable and empty-as-invalid behavior.

openrouter_key_authority.missing_recognized_runtime_secret: non-fatal provider-unavailable: startup may continue and bind; provider-backed operations unavailable/diagnosable
openrouter_key_authority.authority: CONSTITUTION.md lines 104-106; internal/resofeed/runtime_secret.go ResolveOpenRouterRuntimeSecretOptional and requireRuntimeSecretValue
